title: LeWorldModel: Stable End-to-End Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture from Pixels
problem: Joint Embedding Predictive Architectures can learn compact latent world models, but a plain future-embedding prediction loss admits a collapsed solution where the encoder maps many observations to nearly identical representations.

Existing anti-collapse recipes often depend on stop-gradient targets, exponential moving averages, frozen pretrained encoders, reconstruction losses, reward signals, privileged state, or many hand-balanced loss terms.

Reward-free offline control from raw pixels needs a latent space that is both predictable enough for dynamics modeling and compact enough for fast planning, without depending on task-specific supervision.
Prior end-to-end latent dynamics models make hyperparameter tuning and stability difficult, especially when the encoder and predictor are trained jointly from pixels.
solution: LeWM trains an encoder and action-conditioned transformer predictor jointly from offline pixel-action trajectories: frames become compact latent embeddings, and the predictor forecasts the next latent state rather than reconstructing pixels (**4a**, **4b**, 5a).

To prevent collapse, the method adds SIGReg, which projects batches of latent embeddings onto random one-dimensional directions and applies a differentiable Epps-Pulley normality statistic so the latent distribution approaches an isotropic Gaussian (**4c**, 5a).

The full training objective is only next-embedding prediction plus a weighted SIGReg term, so the design removes pretrained target encoders, EMA, stop-gradient updates, reward prediction, and reconstruction losses (**4d**, 5a, 5b).

For decision making, LeWM encodes the current observation and goal observation, rolls out candidate action sequences in latent space, and uses CEM/MPC to minimize the terminal latent distance to the goal (**4e**, **4f**, 5c).

Because planning operates over a compact learned embedding rather than high-resolution visual tokens, the same latent world model can support task-agnostic control while keeping the inference loop simple.
key_concepts: Prediction alone is not enough for a JEPA world model: if every observation shares the same embedding, the next-embedding loss can be small while the representation contains no useful physical state. LeWM makes anti-collapse an explicit distributional constraint instead of relying on target-network heuristics.

SIGReg turns high-dimensional Gaussian matching into many one-dimensional normality tests. The Cramer-Wold perspective makes this meaningful: if all projected marginals match the standard normal, the joint latent distribution is pushed toward an isotropic Gaussian, encouraging feature diversity without covariance-specific loss engineering.

The encoder is a ViT that uses a projected [CLS] token as the compact state, while the predictor is an action-conditioned transformer. AdaLN injects actions progressively, so latent dynamics can be learned from sequences of raw observations and actions without reconstructing images.

The complete LeWM loss leaves one effective balance parameter, the SIGReg weight. This is the paper's main simplification over multi-term end-to-end alternatives: stable joint training comes from a prediction term for dynamics and a principled normality regularizer for representation support.

Latent planning treats the learned representation as the control state. The terminal goal-matching cost is meaningful only if the latent geometry preserves controllable physical structure, so the training objective and planning procedure are tightly coupled.

visuals.caption: LeWorldModel training pipeline
visuals.description: Shows raw observations mapped by the encoder to latent states, action-conditioned prediction of the next latent state, MSE prediction loss, and SIGReg anti-collapse regularization through random projected normality tests.

visuals.caption: Characteristics of latent world model approaches
visuals.description: Positions LeWM in the latent world model design space by contrasting end-to-end, foundation-based, and task-specific approaches and highlighting the method's end-to-end, task-agnostic, pixel-based, reward-free, reconstruction-free design.

visuals.caption: Latent planning with LeWorldModel
visuals.description: Shows inference-time planning: encode the initial and goal observations, roll out predicted latent states over candidate actions, compute a terminal latent cost, and iteratively update actions with a solver.
